name: Motilla del Azuer – circular desde Manzanares
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Manzanares are situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, encompassing the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features diverse landscapes, including the rugged granite formations of La Pedriza Natural Park, sculpted by natural forces. The Manzanares River meanders through the area, contributing to the varied terrain suitable for mountain biking. This combination of mountains, protected parkland, and river valleys provides a range of routes for outdoor enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around Manzanares The most popular mountain bike trail is Motilla del Azuer – circular desde Manzanares, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the Manzanares landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Rezuelo Mill – Membrilla loop from Manzanares, a moderate 34.7 miles (55.9 km) path. This route explores the rural surroundings and river areas near Manzanares. Local mountain bikers also love the El Rezuelo Mill – Valdepeñas loop from Manzanares, a 42.0 miles (67.6 km) trail leading through the open countryside and agricultural lands, often completed in about 4 hours 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Manzanares is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, granite rock formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Manzanares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 170 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manzanares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Manzanares?
A: Manzanares offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, primarily categorized as moderate to difficult. The region, nestled within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains and encompassing the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Park, features rugged granite formations in La Pedriza Natural Park and river valleys. You'll find routes that cater to various skill levels, from those exploring rural surroundings to more challenging paths with technical sections.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Manzanares?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails available in Manzanares, with 33 routes rated as moderate and 4 as difficult. This extensive network provides plenty of options for exploring the region's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in Manzanares?
A: While many trails in Manzanares are rated moderate to difficult, the region's diverse landscape means there are likely flatter sections or less technical paths suitable for families. For a more relaxed ride, consider exploring areas along the Manzanares River banks or the Manzanares Canal Greenway, which offers an easy, paved cycling route. Always check individual route details for suitability before heading out.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike trails in Manzanares?
A: Mountain bike trails in Manzanares vary significantly in length and duration. For example, a moderate trail like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036660379/motilla-del-azuer-circular-desde-manzanares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motilla del Azuer – circular desde Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; is about 23.4 miles (37.6 km) and takes around 2 hours 28 minutes. Longer routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rezuelo Mill – Valdepeñas loop from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;, stretch to 42.0 miles (67.6 km) and can take nearly 5 hours to complete, leading through open countryside.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails in Manzanares?
A: The entire Manzanares region is known for its scenic beauty, particularly within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains and the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Park. Trails often pass through the unique granite formations of La Pedriza Natural Park and along the Manzanares River. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14657822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rezuelo Mill – Membrilla loop from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; offer views of rural surroundings and river areas, providing a picturesque riding experience.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Manzanares?
A: You'll encounter a rich variety of natural features, including the rugged granite rock formations of La Pedriza Natural Park, sculpted by wind and water. The Manzanares River meanders through the area, with its upper reaches forming the Reserva Natural Fluvial del Río Manzanares, known for its granite berrocales and spectacular rocky canyons. You might also discover numerous streams and waterfalls, such as the Covacho Waterfall, enhancing the natural beauty of your ride.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails in Manzanares?
A: Yes, the region offers several interesting landmarks. You can visit the well-preserved 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5085378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; (Manzanares el Real Castle) with panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3569390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Snows&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3960158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest. The Santillana Reservoir, adjacent to the castle, provides a tranquil setting for various activities and birdwatching.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Manzanares?
A: The mountain bike trails in Manzanares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 170 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, scenic beauty, and the options available for different skill levels within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Manzanares?
A: Manzanares offers good mountain biking opportunities throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in exposed areas, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially some snow in higher elevations of the Sierra de Guadarrama, which might affect trail conditions. Always check the local forecast before planning your ride.

Q: Are there any loop routes for mountain biking in Manzanares?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Manzanares are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036660379/motilla-del-azuer-circular-desde-manzanares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motilla del Azuer – circular desde Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38181431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Level crossing S/B EL Carrerón – Camino de Griñón loop from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices among local riders.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails in Manzanares that are less crowded?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, the extensive network of trails in Manzanares, especially those venturing deeper into the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Park or away from the immediate vicinity of Manzanares el Real, can offer a more secluded experience. Exploring routes during off-peak hours or weekdays can also help you find quieter trails.
tips:
Daimiel offers us spectacular surroundings, and the Motilla site does not escape this spell. Over four thousand years ago, its inhabitants built several concentric lines of walls over a deep well and a vigorous square tower.
The Bronze Age emerges in La Mancha lands where water is of great importance due to its scarcity and this dates back to ancient times, so inside the patio the inhabitants of Azuer dug a well until reaching the water table, at a depth of about 16 meters, to supply water to the settlement. Its functionality experienced variations during the different phases of occupation, being also used as an occasional stabling area for sheep, goats and pigs, but mainly as a cereal store (barley and wheat). The grave goods are scarce, except in exceptional cases of adult individuals buried with ceramic vases, daggers with copper rivets and awls of this same metal, which could indicate a hierarchical society. This profound journey to the second millennium BC is not only well preserved but also magical and unique.

La Motilla del Azuer is located on the La Mancha plain, in the municipality of Daimiel (Ciudad Real), and is very close to the Azuer River, which is no coincidence: its location was strategically chosen to guarantee access to groundwater during the Bronze Age.
Camino del Carrerón, which takes us to the Motilla del Azuer Site.

waypoints.name: Crossing of the path with the Azuer River
